title: Two-Clue Chain I
clues: A sits third to the right of B.
C sits immediately to the left of A.
explanation: Use B as an anchor. Place A three positions to B's right, then place C immediately to A's left.
hints: Start with the clue that links B and A.
After placing B and A, use A as the reference for C.
The final relative order is B ... C A, with A three positions right of B.

title: Two-Clue Chain II
clues: Two persons sit between P and Q.
R sits immediately to the right of P.
explanation: P and Q must be three positions apart. R must occupy the immediate-right position of P. Either P-Q orientation is allowed if both clues remain true.
hints: First create a distance of three between P and Q.
Now treat P as the reference for R.
R must be one position to P's right.

title: Three-Clue Chain I
clues: E sits second to the right of D.
F sits immediately to the left of E.
G sits third to the right of F.
explanation: Build the connected chain one clue at a time. D anchors E, E anchors F, and F anchors G.
hints: Clues 1 and 2 share E, so combine them first.
Once F is fixed relative to E, use F to place G.
A valid relative pattern is D F E _ G.

title: Three-Clue Gap Chain
clues: Three persons sit between H and J.
K sits immediately to the right of H.
L sits second to the left of J.
explanation: First establish H and J four positions apart. Then attach K to H and L to J. More than one mirror orientation can be valid.
hints: The H-J gap is the backbone.
Attach K immediately to H's right.
Place L two positions to J's left and verify no token collision occurs.

title: Derived Inference I
clues: N sits fourth to the right of M.
O sits second to the left of N.

derivedQuestion.prompt: After plotting, where is O relative to M?

explanation: N is +4 from M and O is -2 from N, so O is +2 from M.
hints: Plot the two clues first.
Compare O directly with M after the board is correct.
The net movement from M is +4 - 2 = +2.

title: Derived Inference II
clues: T sits third to the right of S.
U sits second to the right of T.
V sits immediately to the left of U.

derivedQuestion.prompt: How many positions are there between S and V?

explanation: Relative to S: T is +3, U is +5, and V is +4. Therefore three positions lie strictly between S and V.
hints: Complete the chain before answering.
Find the positional distance between S and V.
People/positions between equals positional distance minus one.

title: Constraint Integration I
clues: A sits fifth to the right of B.
Two persons sit between C and A.
D sits immediately to the right of B.
explanation: Anchor B and A first because their directional relationship is fixed. Then place D from B. C can occupy either side of A at distance three, provided the board remains valid.
hints: The B-A clue is the strongest directional backbone.
Attach D to B before resolving C.
C has a distance relation, so test only positions exactly three away from A.

title: Reference Discipline
clues: X sits fourth to the left of W.
Y sits second to the right of X.
Z sits immediately to the left of Y.

derivedQuestion.prompt: Where is Z relative to W?

explanation: Let W be 0. X is -4, Y is -2, and Z is -3. Therefore Z is third to the left of W.
hints: Keep the reference person explicit for every clue.
Build X from W, then Y from X, then Z from Y.
Compare Z with W only after the chain is complete.
